pages.text: “ “What’s for supper ?” asked Sam. “It’s a surprise ”, said Mama, putting a big pot on the stove.

blocks.text: “What’s for supper ?” asked Sam.
“It’s a surprise ”, said Mama, putting a big pot on the stove.

pages.text: She added a jug of water . Sam was worried. “Are we having water for supper?” he wailed. “It’s a surprise. Wait and see.” replied Mama.

blocks.text: She added a jug of water . Sam was worried. “Are we having water for supper?” he wailed.
“It’s a surprise. Wait and see.” replied Mama.

pages.text: “Can I help ?” asked Sam. “I could stir .” “You are still a bit short ,” said Mama. “The pot might fall over. But you can help me by fetching things.”

blocks.text: “Can I help ?” asked Sam. “I could stir .”
“You are still a bit short ,” said Mama. “The pot might fall over. But you can help me by fetching things.”

pages.text: “Can you bring me an onion , please.” “This doesn’t smell very nice.” “They will taste nice once they are cooked .”

blocks.text: “Can you bring me an onion , please.”
“This doesn’t smell very nice.”
“They will taste nice once they are cooked .”

pages.text: “Now I need some tomatoes . No, that is an apple . The tomatoes are also round and red, but you will find them in the fridge.”

blocks.text: “Now I need some tomatoes . No, that is an apple . The tomatoes are also round and red, but you will find them in the fridge.”

pages.text: “Can you bring me potatoes . They are round and brown .” “Is supper ready yet?”

blocks.text: “Can you bring me potatoes . They are round and brown .”
“Is supper ready yet?”

pages.text: “If you are hungry you can eat the apple while you wait.” “What about these?” “No, we won’t need biscuits. We can save those for after supper .”

blocks.text: “If you are hungry you can eat the apple while you wait.”
“What about these?”
“No, we won’t need biscuits. We can save those for after supper .”

pages.text: “What’s in the pot ?” “It’s a surprise . Wait and see.”

blocks.text: “What’s in the pot ?”
“It’s a surprise . Wait and see.”

pages.text: “What about this ?” “That is a watermelon . It will be too big for the pot.” “See if you can find some spinach . It is also green .”

blocks.text: “What about this ?”
“That is a watermelon . It will be too big for the pot.”
“See if you can find some spinach . It is also green .”

pages.text: “Please get some bread. No, it won’t go into the pot but on the table .”

blocks.text: “Please get some bread.
No, it won’t go into the pot but on the table .”

pages.text: Mama carried the pot to the table and started to dish up. “What’s in the pot ?” asked Sam.

blocks.text: Mama carried the pot to the table and started to dish up. “What’s in the pot ?” asked Sam.
My “Soup!” favourite!
